description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Water meters are being stolen across the country. Hanno City asks residents to manage their meters properly.

Check your meter regularly, including for leaks. If you discover theft or see suspicious activity around a meter box, contact the police or the Water Services Division (phone: 042-973-2111).

【Places where theft is common】
• Apartment buildings
• Vacant houses
• Empty land
• Construction sites at new homes

【About meter readings】
Reaings are done by Daiichi Kankyo Co., Ltd., contracted by Hanno City. Meter readers wear uniforms and carry photo IDs issued by the city. If you have doubts, ask to see their ID.

【About meter replacement】
Water meters have an 8-year expiration date set by law, and we replace them before that date expires. If your meter needs replacement, you will receive a postcard titled "Water Meter Replacement Notice" about one month before the scheduled replacement date. Replacement is done by water service contractors in the city who belong to the Hanno City Water Works Cooperative, and workers wear armbands. If you have doubts, ask to see their armband.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/jogesuidobu/suidogyomuka/13515.html
summary: ℹ️ Watch out for water meter theft
